Description: Embedded Rust uses #![no_std] to compile without the standard library targeting bare-metal systems
Do: Use #![no_std] with #![no_main]; define panic handler; use cortex-m-rt for ARM; use embedded-hal traits for portability
Dont: Use std library features on embedded targets; rely on heap allocation
Code Bad: use std::collections::HashMap;\n// std is not available on embedded targets\n// HashMap requires heap allocation

Guideline: Use embedded-hal traits for hardware abstraction
Description: Program against embedded-hal traits for portable driver code that works across different microcontrollers
Do: Use embedded_hal::digital::OutputPin, embedded_hal::i2c::I2c etc; write drivers generic over HAL traits
Dont: Write drivers for specific chip registers; hardcode pin assignments

Description: Avoid heap allocation in embedded contexts; use static buffers, heapless collections, and const generics
Do: Use heapless::Vec, heapless::String for bounded collections; use static mut with critical sections; use const generics for buffer sizes
Dont: Use Vec or String which require heap allocation; rely on dynamic memory
Code Bad: let mut buffer: Vec<u8> = Vec::new();\nbuffer.push(42); // requires allocator!\n// Vec is not available in #![no_std] without alloc

Description: Embassy provides an async runtime for embedded Rust with zero-allocation task scheduling
Do: Use embassy-executor for async tasks; embassy-time for timers; embassy-stm32/nrf for HAL; #[embassy_executor::task] for concurrent tasks
Dont: Use busy-wait loops for multi-tasking; implement custom schedulers

Guideline: Test embedded code on host with mocks
Description: Use embedded-hal-mock to test driver code on the host machine without hardware
Do: Write drivers generic over embedded-hal traits; test with mocks on x86; use defmt for embedded logging
Dont: Only test on real hardware; skip testing embedded code entirely
Code Bad: // Only testing on real hardware:\n// Must flash device, connect probe, read UART output\n// Cannot run in CI, cannot automate
